children.heading: Administrator
children.label: 1 Administrator
children.text: The term “Administrator” means the Administrator of General Services.

children.heading: Advisory Committee
children.label: 2 Advisory Committee
children.text: The term “Advisory Committee” means the Green Building Advisory Committee established under section 484.1

children.heading: Commercial Director
children.label: 3 Commercial Director
children.text: The term “Commercial Director” means the individual appointed to the position established under .section 17081 of this title

children.heading: Consortium
children.label: 4 Consortium
children.text: The term “Consortium” means the High-Performance Green Building Partnership Consortium created in response to to represent the private sector in a public-private partnership to promote high-performance green buildings and zero-net-energy commercial buildings.section 17092(c)(1) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: will result in substantial operational cost savings by ensuring an installed consumption of not more than 1 watt per square foot; or

children.label: I
children.text: ;section 8259b of this title

children.label: II
children.text: Federal acquisition regulation 23–203; and

children.label: III
children.text: is at least as energy-conserving as required by other provisions of this Act, including the requirements of this title and title III which shall be applicable to the extent that they would achieve greater energy savings than provided under clause (i) or this clause.112So in original. Does not fit with cl. (ii) introductory provision.2

children.label: i
children.text: lamps;

children.label: ii
children.text: ballasts;

children.label: iii
children.text: luminaires;

children.label: iv
children.text: lighting controls;

children.label: v
children.text: daylighting; and

children.label: vi
children.text: early use of other highly cost-effective lighting technologies.

children.label: A
children.text: will result in substantial operational cost savings by reducing electricity or fossil fuel consumption, water, or other utility costs, including use of geothermal heat pumps;

children.label: B
children.text: complies with the provisions of and Federal acquisition regulation 23–203; andsection 8259b of this title

children.label: C
children.text: is at least as energy and water conserving as required under this title, including sections 431 through 435, and title V, including sections 511 through 525, which shall be applicable to the extent that they are more stringent or require greater energy or water savings than required by this section.11

children.heading: Federal Director
children.label: 7 Federal Director
children.text: The term “Federal Director” means the individual appointed to the position established under .section 17092(a) of this title

children.heading: Federal facility
children.label: 8 Federal facility
children.text: The term “Federal facility” means any building that is constructed, renovated, leased, or purchased in part or in whole for use by the Federal Government.

children.label: i
children.text: for cost-effective technologies and practices, the date that is 5 years after the date of installation; and

children.label: ii
children.text: for geothermal heat pumps, as soon as practical after the date of installation of the applicable geothermal heat pump.

children.label: i
children.text: the installation or use of cost-effective technologies and practices; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the planting of vegetation that shades the facility and reduces the heating, cooling, or lighting needs of the facility.

children.heading: Exclusion
children.label: C Exclusion
children.text: The term “operational cost savings” does not include savings from measures that would likely be adopted in the absence of cost-effective technology and practices programs, as determined by the Administrator.

children.label: A
children.text: uses the ground or ground water as a thermal energy source to heat, or as a thermal energy sink to cool, a building; and

children.label: B
children.text: meets the requirements of the Energy Star program of the Environmental Protection Agency applicable to geothermal heat pumps on the date of purchase of the technology.

children.label: i
children.text: is constructed (including facilities constructed for lease), renovated, or purchased, in whole or in part, by the Administrator for use by the Federal Government; or

children.label: I
children.text: except as provided in subclause (II), for a term of not less than 5 years; or

children.label: II
children.text: for a term of less than 5 years, if the Administrator determines that use of cost-effective technologies and practices would result in the payback of expenses.

children.heading: Inclusion
children.label: B Inclusion
children.text: The term “GSA facility” includes any group of buildings, structures, or facilities described in subparagraph (A) (including the associated energy-consuming support systems of the buildings, structures, and facilities).

children.heading: Exemption
children.label: C Exemption
children.text: The Administrator may exempt from the definition of “GSA facility” under this paragraph a building, structure, or facility that meets the requirements of .section 8253(c) of this title

children.heading: High-performance building
children.label: 12 High-performance building
children.text: The term “high-performance building” means a building that integrates and optimizes on a life cycle basis all major high performance attributes, including energy conservation, environment, safety, security, durability, accessibility, cost-benefit, productivity, sustainability, functionality, and operational considerations.

children.label: A
children.text: reduces energy, water, and material resource use;

children.label: B
children.text: improves indoor environmental quality, including reducing indoor pollution, improving thermal comfort, and improving lighting and acoustic environments that affect occupant health and productivity;

children.label: C
children.text: reduces negative impacts on the environment throughout the life-cycle of the building, including air and water pollution and waste generation;

children.label: D
children.text: increases the use of environmentally preferable products, including biobased, recycled content, and nontoxic products with lower life-cycle impacts;

children.label: E
children.text: increases reuse and recycling opportunities;

children.label: F
children.text: integrates systems in the building;

children.label: G
children.text: reduces the environmental and energy impacts of transportation through building location and site design that support a full range of transportation choices for users of the building; and

children.label: i
children.text: improvements in worker productivity;

children.label: ii
children.text: the life-cycle impacts of building materials and operations; and

children.label: iii
children.text: other factors that the Federal Director or the Commercial Director consider to be appropriate.

children.heading: Life-cycle
children.label: 14 Life-cycle
children.text: The term “life-cycle”, with respect to a high-performance green building, means all stages of the useful life of the building (including components, equipment, systems, and controls of the building) beginning at conception of a high-performance green building project and continuing through site selection, design, construction, landscaping, commissioning, operation, maintenance, renovation, deconstruction or demolition, removal, and recycling of the high-performance green building.

children.heading: Life-cycle assessment
children.label: 15 Life-cycle assessment
children.text: The term “life-cycle assessment” means a comprehensive system approach for measuring the environmental performance of a product or service over the life of the product or service, beginning at raw materials acquisition and continuing through manufacturing, transportation, installation, use, reuse, and end-of-life waste management.

children.label: A
children.text: sums, over a given study period, the costs of initial investment (less resale value), replacements, operations (including energy use), and maintenance and repair of an investment decision; and

children.label: i
children.text: in present value terms, in the case of a study period equivalent to the longest useful life of the building, determined by taking into consideration the typical life of such a building in the area in which the building is to be located; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in annual value terms, in the case of any other study period.

children.heading: Office of Commercial High-Performance Green Buildings
children.label: 17 Office of Commercial High-Performance Green Buildings
children.text: The term “Office of Commercial High-Performance Green Buildings” means the Office of Commercial High-Performance Green Buildings established under .section 17081(a) of this title

children.heading: Office of Federal High-Performance Green Buildings
children.label: 18 Office of Federal High-Performance Green Buildings
children.text: The term “Office of Federal High-Performance Green Buildings” means the Office of Federal High-Performance Green Buildings established under .section 17092(a) of this title

children.heading: Practices
children.label: 19 Practices
children.text: The term “practices” means design, financing, permitting, construction, commissioning, operation and maintenance, and other practices that contribute to achieving zero-net-energy buildings or facilities.

children.label: A
children.text: require a greatly reduced quantity of energy to operate;

children.label: B
children.text: meet the balance of energy needs from sources of energy that do not produce greenhouse gases;

children.label: C
children.text: therefore result in no net emissions of greenhouse gases; and

children.label: D
children.text: be economically viable.
